query: What is the official currency of Mexico?
answer: The official currency of Mexico is the Mexican Peso (MXN). It is subdivided into 100 centavos. The currency symbol is $.

results.title: Currency in Mexico | A Travelers Guide to Visiting Mexico
results.snippet: The official currency of Mexico is the Mexican Peso (MXN), represented by the “$” sign. One peso is divided into 100 centavos, much like cents in the US dollar.

results.title: MXN | Mexican peso - Oanda
results.snippet: # Mexican Peso Currency. # MXN. The Mexican peso (MXN) is the official currency of Mexico. It is subdivided into 100 centavos. The currency was initially based on Spain’s official currency, called ‘real’, which was the Spanish dollar. The Mexican peso’s name originated from the silver 8-real coins issued by Spain in Mexico. | Nicknames | Varos, morlacos, lana, papiros, marimba, varonil |. | Central bank | Bank of Mexico |. | Currency subunits | Centavo = 1/100 |. | Denominations | Banknotes: $20, $50, $100, $200, $500, $1,000 Coins: 5¢, 10¢, 20¢, 50¢, $1, $2, $5, $10, $20, $50, $100 |. | Countries using this currency | Mexico |. | Currencies pegged to MXN | None |. | MXN is pegged to | None |. ## Looking for the Currency Converter? ## Ready to send money? ## Footer. ### Currency Converter. ### FX Payments. © 2026 OANDA Business Information and Services Inc. results.title: 5 Currency Facts You Probably Didn't Know About The Mexican Peso - Currency Exchange International, Corp.
results.snippet: # 5 Currency Facts You Probably Didn't Know About The Mexican Peso. 5 Currency Facts You Probably Didn't Know About The Mexican Peso. The Mexican Peso is the official currency of Mexico, denoted by the $ symbol and currency code MXN. So, when you're holding a modern peso in your hand, you're not just holding Mexican currency, but a direct descendant of one of the world's most historically influential forms of money. It's believed the symbol evolved from an abbreviation of "Ps" for pesos, where the "S" was written over the "P." This eventually simplified into the dollar-like "$." The United States later adopted it for their own currency in the late 1700s, borrowing from a system already in widespread use in North America. Despite being a currency for a single country, the Mexican peso ranks in the top 20 most-traded currencies globally, often cracking the top 10.

results.title: Currency in Mexico: A Complete Guide - Wise
results.snippet: The currency in Mexico is the Mexican peso. Each peso is divided into 100 cents. When you're buying currency for Mexico, look out for the currency code MXN.

results.title: Introduction to Mexico's Peso, its Coins & Banknotes - Mexperience
results.snippet: Mexico's official currency is the Mexican peso. There are one hundred Mexican cents (centavos) to every peso.
